summary: A minimal React/TypeScript starter template demonstrating how to build a single-page application on top of the Palantir Foundry Ontology SDK (OSDK). The app shows the complete OAuth2 authentication flow using PublicClientAuth, client initialization via FoundryClient, and paginated object querying through the generated ontology client. It uses a 'Country' object type as a sample, demonstrating the pattern of accessing ontology objects via `client.ontology.objects.<ObjectType>.page()` with typed Result/Error handling. The SDK is distributed as a pre-built tarball (sdk-0.14.0.tgz) installed from a Foundry-hosted NPM registry, with generated TypeScript types for ontology objects.

ontologyConcepts.evidence: The app imports and queries a 'Country' object type from the generated SDK package '@ontology-starter/sdk/ontology/objects'. Objects are accessed via `client.ontology.objects.Country.page({ pageSize: 10 })`, which returns a paginated result of Country instances. Each object has a `__primaryKey` property and typed properties like `countryName`.

The Country object type exposes typed properties accessed directly on the object instance. The home page renders `object.countryName` (a string property) and `object.__primaryKey` (the built-in primary key property). These properties are generated from the Ontology definition and provide full TypeScript type safety.

Authentication is handled through PublicClientAuth (OAuth2 public client flow) with configurable clientId, API URL, and redirect URL. The app implements a complete auth flow: login page triggers `client.auth.signIn()` which redirects to Foundry for authentication, the callback page completes the flow, and an AuthenticatedRoute guard checks `client.auth.token` validity and refreshes expired tokens via `client.auth.refresh()`. Environment variables FOUNDRY_CLIENT_ID, FOUNDRY_API_URL, and APPLICATION_REDIRECT_URL configure the auth parameters. CORS must be configured in Foundry's Control Panel.

Not directly demonstrated in this starter app. The SDK infrastructure supports link types through the ontology namespace but the Country example only shows flat object querying without traversing links between object types.
Not directly demonstrated in this starter app. The SDK likely supports actions through the client.ontology namespace but no action invocations are shown. The app is read-only, only fetching objects.
Not demonstrated in this starter app. No function invocations are present in the codebase.

The SDK is distributed as a pre-built tarball (sdk-0.14.0.tgz) generated from the Foundry Developer Console. Installation requires a Foundry API token for the application-specific NPM registry. The webpack dev server can proxy API calls to the Foundry instance to avoid CORS issues during development, with the proxy routing '/multipass/api/**' and '/api/**' paths.
Not present in this starter app.
Not directly demonstrated. The SDK may support ontology interfaces but the starter app only uses concrete object types.

apiSurface.name: FoundryClient
apiSurface.description: The root client class for interacting with the Foundry Ontology SDK. Initialized with a configuration object containing `url` (Foundry API URL) and `auth` (authentication provider). Provides access to ontology objects, authentication, and other Foundry APIs. Imported from the generated SDK package (e.g., '@ontology-starter/sdk').

apiSurface.name: PublicClientAuth
apiSurface.description: OAuth2 public client authentication provider for browser-based SPAs. Configured with `clientId`, `url` (Foundry URL), and `redirectUrl`. Exposes `signIn()` to initiate OAuth redirect flow, `refresh()` to refresh expired tokens, and a `token` property with `isExpired` check. Designed for client-facing applications (public clients, not confidential).

apiSurface.name: client.ontology.objects.<ObjectType>.page()
apiSurface.description: Fetches a paginated list of ontology objects of a given type. Accepts options like `{ pageSize: number }`. Returns `Result<Page<T>, ListObjectsError>` where T is the typed object (e.g., Country). The Page result contains a `data` array of typed object instances.

apiSurface.name: Result<T, E>
apiSurface.description: A discriminated union type representing success or failure. Checked using `isOk(result)` for success (accessing `result.value`) or `isErr(result)` for error (accessing `result.error`). Part of the SDK's error-handling pattern.

apiSurface.name: isOk / isErr
apiSurface.description: Type guard functions for the Result type. `isOk(result)` narrows to success variant with `.value`, `isErr(result)` narrows to error variant with `.error`.

apiSurface.name: Page<T>
apiSurface.description: Represents a paginated result containing a `data` array of typed ontology objects. Returned from `.page()` calls on object type namespaces.

apiSurface.name: ListObjectsError
apiSurface.description: Error type for object listing operations. Contains discriminated error variants like `ObjectTypeNotFound` with an `objectType` field. Handled via the ErrorVisitor pattern.

apiSurface.name: ErrorVisitor<E, R>
apiSurface.description: A visitor interface for structured error handling. Maps error variant names to handler functions plus a `default` catch-all. Used with `visitError(error, visitor)` to pattern-match on specific error types.

apiSurface.name: visitError
apiSurface.description: Applies an ErrorVisitor to an error value, dispatching to the matching handler function based on the error's variant tag. Provides exhaustive, type-safe error handling.

apiSurface.name: client.auth.signIn()
apiSurface.description: Initiates the OAuth2 authorization flow. On first call, redirects the user to Foundry's login page. On the callback route, completes the flow by exchanging the authorization code for tokens and storing them.

apiSurface.name: client.auth.refresh()
apiSurface.description: Attempts to refresh an expired authentication token. Returns a promise that resolves on success or rejects if no valid session exists (user needs to re-authenticate).

apiSurface.name: client.auth.token
apiSurface.description: The current authentication token object. May be null if not authenticated. Has an `isExpired` boolean property to check token validity.

apiSurface.name: Country
apiSurface.description: A generated TypeScript interface representing the 'Country' ontology object type. Imported from '@ontology-starter/sdk/ontology/objects'. Has a `__primaryKey` property and domain-specific properties like `countryName`. Serves as the sample object type in the starter template.

architecturePatterns.description: The Ontology SDK is generated per-application via the Foundry Developer Console and distributed as a pre-built npm tarball (sdk-0.14.0.tgz) bundled directly in the repo under `sdk/`. It provides fully typed access to the application's ontology, with object types importable from subpath exports like '@ontology-starter/sdk/ontology/objects'. Installation requires a Foundry API token for the app-specific NPM registry.

Authentication uses the OAuth2 authorization code flow for public clients (browser SPAs). The flow is: LoginPage calls `client.auth.signIn()` which redirects to Foundry -> user authenticates -> redirected to `/auth/callback` -> AuthCallback component calls `signIn()` again to complete the token exchange -> navigates to protected home page. An AuthenticatedRoute wrapper guards protected routes by checking token validity and attempting refresh before falling back to login redirect.

API calls return a Result<T, E> type rather than throwing exceptions. Code uses `isOk()`/`isErr()` type guards to discriminate success from failure, and an ErrorVisitor pattern with `visitError()` for exhaustive, type-safe error handling with named error variants and a default fallback.

Ontology objects are accessed through a hierarchical namespace chain on the client: `client.ontology.objects.<ObjectType>.<method>()`. This provides a strongly-typed, discoverable API surface where each object type in the ontology becomes a named property on the objects namespace, with query methods like `.page()` available on each.

All Foundry connection parameters (API URL, client ID, redirect URL) are injected via environment variables using dotenv-webpack. Separate .env files for development and production, with a no-CORS variant for environments where CORS policy cannot be modified. The webpack dev server can proxy API requests to avoid CORS issues during local development.

Uses react-router-dom v6 with a layout route pattern where AuthenticatedRoute acts as a parent route element using <Outlet/> to render child routes. Unauthenticated users are redirected to /login, and the login page redirects authenticated users back to /. The /auth/callback route handles the OAuth redirect completion.

typeDefinitions.name: Country
typeDefinitions.definition: interface Country { __primaryKey: string; countryName: string; /* ...other ontology-defined properties */ }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Generated TypeScript interface representing the 'Country' ontology object type. Imported from the generated SDK at '@ontology-starter/sdk/ontology/objects'. Provides type-safe access to ontology object properties. The `__primaryKey` is a built-in field present on all ontology objects.

typeDefinitions.name: Result<T, E>
typeDefinitions.purpose: Discriminated union for API operation outcomes. Success variant accessed via `isOk()` guard with `.value: T`, error variant via `isErr()` guard with `.error: E`. Prevents unchecked error handling by making failure explicit in the type system.

typeDefinitions.name: Page<T>
typeDefinitions.definition: interface Page<T> { data: T[]; /* pagination metadata */ }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Paginated result container for object list queries. Contains a `data` array of typed ontology objects returned from `.page()` API calls.

typeDefinitions.name: ListObjectsError
typeDefinitions.purpose: Union of error types that can occur when listing ontology objects. The ObjectTypeNotFound variant includes an `objectType` field identifying which type was not found. Used with ErrorVisitor for typed error handling.

typeDefinitions.name: ErrorVisitor<E, R>
typeDefinitions.definition: type ErrorVisitor<E, R> = { [K in ErrorVariant<E>]: (err: Extract<E, { type: K }>) => R } & { default: () => R }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Visitor pattern type for exhaustive error handling. Maps each error variant name to a handler function, plus a required `default` catch-all. Enforces that all error cases are considered.

typeDefinitions.name: FoundryClient config
typeDefinitions.purpose: Configuration object for FoundryClient constructor. The `url` is the Foundry API base URL and `auth` is the authentication provider instance.

typeDefinitions.name: PublicClientAuth config
typeDefinitions.definition: { clientId: string; url: string; redirectUrl: string }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Configuration for OAuth2 public client authentication. The `clientId` is from the Foundry Developer Console, `url` is the Foundry instance URL, and `redirectUrl` is the OAuth callback URL.

typeDefinitions.name: Token
typeDefinitions.definition: interface Token { isExpired: boolean; /* token data */ }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Authentication token type exposed via `client.auth.token`. Used to check if the user is authenticated and whether the token needs refreshing. May be null when no session exists.

dependencies.name: @ontology-starter/sdk
dependencies.purpose: Generated Ontology SDK providing typed access to Foundry ontology objects, authentication (FoundryClient, PublicClientAuth), error handling (Result, ErrorVisitor), and object querying. Distributed as a bundled tarball (sdk-0.14.0.tgz) generated from the Foundry Developer Console.

dependencies.name: react
dependencies.purpose: Core UI framework for building the single-page application.

dependencies.name: react-dom
dependencies.purpose: React DOM renderer, using createRoot API for React 18 concurrent mode.

dependencies.name: react-router-dom
dependencies.purpose: Client-side routing with createBrowserRouter, supporting layout routes for authentication guards and the OAuth callback route.

dependencies.name: querystring-es3
dependencies.purpose: Browser-compatible querystring polyfill used by the OSDK for URL parameter handling. Configured as a webpack fallback for the Node.js 'querystring' module.

dependencies.name: stream-browserify
dependencies.purpose: Browser-compatible stream polyfill, likely required by the OSDK's HTTP client internals.

dependencies.name: tslib
dependencies.purpose: TypeScript runtime helpers library, used with importHelpers compiler option to reduce bundle size.

dependencies.name: typescript
dependencies.purpose: TypeScript compiler for type checking and transpilation.

dependencies.name: webpack
dependencies.purpose: Module bundler for building the production bundle. Configured with ts-loader, sass-loader, dotenv-webpack, and HTML template plugin.

dependencies.name: webpack-dev-server
dependencies.purpose: Development server with hot reloading. Can proxy API requests to the Foundry instance to bypass CORS during local development.

dependencies.name: dotenv-webpack
dependencies.purpose: Webpack plugin that injects environment variables from .env files into the bundle via process.env references.
